---
title: "ARE"
format: html
editor: visual
---
```{r}
if (!requireNamespace("BiocManager", quietly = TRUE))
    install.packages("BiocManager")

BiocManager::install(c(
  "biomaRt",
  "GenomicRanges",
  "JASPAR2022",
  "TFBSTools",
  "motifmatchr",
  "BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38"
))

library(biomaRt)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(JASPAR2022)
library(TFBSTools)
library(motifmatchr)
library(BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38)
```


```{r}
your_gene_list <- toupper(c(
  "GAP",      # confirm this
  "CHD7",
  "DGKQ",
  "NIM1K",
  "RPS11",
  "UREC1",    # confirm this
  "RPL8",
  "RPS18",
  "RPLP0",
  "RPS7",
  "RPL12",
  "ABT",      # confirm this
  "RPS10",
  "UBB",
  "RPL17",
  "FASN",
  "FABP6",
  "RBP7",
  "WNK3",
  "ARF1",
  "PLBD2",
  "HSPB11",
  "AHSG"
))

# Standardise to uppercase (BioMart expects this for human)
your_gene_list <- toupper(your_gene_list)
```

```{r}
# Connect to Ensembl
#mart_sparvus <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl")

mart_sparvus <- useMart("ensembl", 
                       dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl",
                       host = "https://useast.ensembl.org")  # US mirror

# If you have gene symbols
human_genes <- getBM(
  attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id", "hgnc_symbol"),
  filters = "hgnc_symbol",
  values = your_gene_list, # your vector of S. parvus gene names/symbols
  mart = mart_sparvus
)
```

```{r}
mart_human <- useMart("ensembl", 
                       dataset = "hsapiens_gene_ensembl",
                       host = "https://useast.ensembl.org")  # US mirror

# Get TSS coordinates
tss <- getBM(
  attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id", 
                 "hgnc_symbol",
                 "chromosome_name",
                 "transcription_start_site", 
                 "strand"),
  filters = "hgnc_symbol",
  values = human_genes$hgnc_symbol,
  mart = mart_human
)

# Keep standard chromosomes only
tss <- tss[tss$chromosome_name %in% c(1:22, "X", "Y"), ]

# Build promoter ranges (2000bp upstream, 200bp downstream of TSS)
promoter_ranges <- GRanges(
  seqnames = paste0("chr", tss$chromosome_name),
  ranges = IRanges(
    start = ifelse(tss$strand == 1,
                   tss$transcription_start_site - 2000,
                   tss$transcription_start_site - 200),
    end = ifelse(tss$strand == 1,
                 tss$transcription_start_site + 200,
                 tss$transcription_start_site + 2000)
  ),
  strand = ifelse(tss$strand == 1, "+", "-"),
  gene = tss$hgnc_symbol
)
```

```{r}
genome <- BSgenome.Hsapiens.UCSC.hg38

sequences <- getSeq(genome, promoter_ranges)
names(sequences) <- promoter_ranges$gene
```

```{r}
library(JASPAR2022)
library(TFBSTools)

# Retrieve all three AR motifs
motifs <- getMatrixSet(
  JASPAR2022,
  list(ID = c("MA0007.1", "MA0007.2", "MA0007.3"))
)

# View basic info for each
motifs

# View the position weight matrix for each
#getPWM(motifs[["MA0007.1"]])
motifs[["MA0007.1"]]

getPWM(motifs[["MA0007.2"]])
getPWM(motifs[["MA0007.3"]])

# View consensus sequence
consensusMatrix(motifs[["MA0007.1"]])

# Get the name/TF associated with each motif
name(motifs[["MA0007.1"]])
name(motifs[["MA0007.2"]])
name(motifs[["MA0007.3"]])
```

```{r}
motifs <- getMatrixSet(
  JASPAR2022,
  list(ID = c("MA0007.1", "MA0007.2", "MA0007.3"))
)
```

```{r}
motif_hits <- matchMotifs(
  motifs,
  sequences,
  out = "positions",
  p.cutoff = 0.0001
)

# Convert to readable dataframe
results <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(motif_hits), function(motif) {
  hits <- as.data.frame(motif_hits[[motif]])
  hits$motif <- motif
  hits$gene <- names(sequences)[hits$group]
  return(hits)
}))

# View genes with at least one ARE hit
ARE_genes <- unique(results$gene)
cat("Genes with predicted AREs:", length(ARE_genes), "\n")
print(ARE_genes)
```

```{r}
write.csv(results, "ARE_hits_human.csv", row.names = FALSE)

# Simple summary table
summary_table <- as.data.frame(table(results$gene))
colnames(summary_table) <- c("gene", "ARE_count")
summary_table <- summary_table[order(-summary_table$ARE_count), ]
write.csv(summary_table, "ARE_summary_human.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# Convert results to GRanges
hits_gr <- GRanges(
  seqnames = results$gene,
  ranges = IRanges(start = results$start, end = results$end),
  motif = results$motif,
  gene = results$gene,
  score = results$score
)

# Split by gene, reduce within each gene, then count
hits_by_gene <- split(hits_gr, results$gene)
reduced_by_gene <- lapply(hits_by_gene, reduce)

# Count unique non-overlapping AREs per gene
unique_counts <- data.frame(
  gene = names(reduced_by_gene),
  unique_ARE_count = sapply(reduced_by_gene, length)
)

# Sort by count
unique_counts <- unique_counts[order(-unique_counts$unique_ARE_count), ]
print(unique_counts)
```
```{r}
# Get maximum motif score per gene
max_scores <- aggregate(score ~ gene, data = results, FUN = max)
colnames(max_scores) <- c("gene", "max_motif_score")

# Merge with your unique counts table
final_table <- merge(unique_counts, max_scores, by = "gene")

# Sort by unique ARE count
final_table <- final_table[order(-final_table$unique_ARE_count), ]

print(final_table)
write.csv(final_table, "ARE_final_table.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```
